idea.hook: Why Lunar Footage Looks So Unusual
facts: The Moon is about 238,855 miles from Earth on average.
Apollo footage still matters because lunar dust, lighting, and motion are hard to fake convincingly.
idea.payoff: The Moon is about 238,855 miles from Earth on average.
idea.title: Why Lunar Footage Looks So Unusual

scenes.text: This is real lunar mission footage, where harsh sunlight, dust, and low gravity make motion look unlike anything on Earth.

The Moon is about 238,855 miles from Earth on average.

leader_kit.pinnedComment: The Moon is about 238,855 miles from Earth on average. What should this lesson explain next?
replyTemplates: The Moon's average distance is about 238,855 miles, which is why even familiar footage still feels enormous.
Apollo visuals hit because the light behaves differently on the Moon than it does in an atmosphere.
